title: Dense Romantic British Egg Scotch Egg
description: A rich and savory Scotch egg filled with a dense, yeast-based mixture, perfect for a romantic breakfast or brunch. The smoky flavor from the bacon and eggs pairs beautifully with the earthy taste of the yeast.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, salt, and sugar; whisk to combine.
Add the softened parsnip and onion to the dry ingredients and mix until well combined.
Gradually add the beaten egg to the mixture, stirring until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5-7 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
While the dough is rising, cook the bacon in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy. Reserve the drippings.
Divide the risen dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Place a large egg in the center of each dough ball and wrap the dough around the egg, pressing the edges together to seal the egg inside.
Dip each Scotch egg in the beaten egg and then roll in the reserved bacon drippings to coat.
Heat the shortening in a large skillet over medium heat until it reaches 375°F. Fry the Scotch eggs in batches until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Drain the Scotch eggs on paper towels and serve warm.
